repos.summary:
The `ref_update` branch was the focus of recent work, where a commit on July 1, 2026, modified the code to "prevent double crossref api, log errors", as stated in the commit message. 
On June 30, 2026, in the same `ref_update` branch, the `ref_accuracy` was updated and validated, according to the commit message. 
Additionally, on June 30, 2026, "Minor fixes to reg_check and ref_consistency" were made, as specified in the commit message. 
No other branches were worked on during this period, with all changes occurring on the `ref_update` branch. 
The `ref_update` branch saw changes to error logging, `ref_accuracy`, `reg_check`, and `ref_consistency`.

recent_commits.message: Merge exact_p_zero_warning: flag p-values reported as exactly zero
recent_commits.body:
Adds detection of p-values reported as exactly zero (e.g. p = .000),
which are mathematically impossible, to the stat_p_exact module, with
dedicated report/guidance text and tests. Resolves a test-file EOL
conflict (content was identical).
recent_commits.author: Lakens

recent_commits.message: Rewrite OSF prereg extraction to schema-driven dispatch
recent_commits.body:
Replace the template-name if/else dispatch and per-template parsers
(osf_pr_28/31, oer, prc, prap, osfpre, rrbrandt) with a single
schema-driven extractor:

- dispatch on the stable registration schema_id, with a dedicated
  handler only for the deeply-nested van 't Veer pages schema
- generic osf_pr_schema() reads field labels from the registration's
  schema (blocks display_text or pages question titles) and maps them
  to canonical prereg_schema fields via osf_label_field, with a slug
  fallback so unknown labels are not dropped
- handles both blocks- and pages-format schemas

Add tests for blocks/pages extraction and the van 't Veer handler, with
OSF guid/registration/schema httptest2 fixtures.
recent_commits.author: Lakens

recent_commits.message: Point home page text to the Metacheck Manual
recent_commits.body:
Update the landing-page sentence to link the Metacheck Manual instead of
the removed Get Started and Articles menu items.
recent_commits.author: Lakens

recent_commits.message: Replace Get Started/Reference/Articles navbar with Metacheck Manual link
recent_commits.body:
Point the site navbar at the new Metacheck Manual book
(https://www.scienceverse.org/metacheck_book/) and drop the
Get Started, Reference, and Articles menu items. Rebuild pkgdown site.
recent_commits.author: Lakens

recent_commits.body:
rtransparent_coi() used the magrittr pipe %>%, which is only available
when dplyr is attached, not when it is merely loaded as module_run() does.
Running coi_check as the first dplyr-importing module in a fresh session
therefore errored. Replace the two %>% with the base pipe |>.

Also simplify the table pipeline in both modules from a
nest_by/rowwise/mutate(data$text) dance to summarise(.by = paper_id),
removing the fragile rowwise data pronoun.
recent_commits.author: Lakens

recent_commits.message: Add R/import-papers.R so dev installs cleanly
recent_commits.body:
The NAMESPACE exports papers_available/papers_load/papers_metadata/
papers_remove, but R/import-papers.R (which defines them) was lost in the
merge from papers_databases, leaving dev uninstallable
(undefined exports). Restore the file.

when building summary_table, which only worked for a single paper and
crashed with "differing number of rows" on a paperlist. repo_check's
file table never carried paper_id, so code_check had no way to
aggregate per paper. Also ensure parse_error/parse_error_msg always
exist with consistent types regardless of file language.

repos.readme_excerpt:
`statcheck` is a “spellchecker” for statistics. It checks whether your
*p*-values match their accompanying test statistic and degrees of
freedom.

`statcheck` searches for null-hypothesis significance test (NHST) in APA
style (e.g., *t*(28) = 2.2, *p* \< .05). It recalculates the p-value
using the reported test statistic and degrees of freedom. If the
reported and computed p-values don’t match, `statcheck` will flag the
result as an error.

![](man/figures/infograph.png)

1.  **Self-checks**: you can use `statcheck` to make sure your

On the `main` branch, a fix was applied to correct "stale PDFextractor/docpluck paths" which were updated to point to the `MetaScienceTools/` layout. Additionally, on the `main` branch, a new feature was added for "region-driven Camelot capture" along with "content-plausibility guards" for `DP-1` and `DP-2`. repos.readme_excerpt:
PDF, DOCX, and HTML text extraction plus normalization for academic papers.

The full documentation lives in `docs/README.md`.


```bash
pip install docpluck
```

Optional extras:

```bash
pip install docpluck[docx]
pip install docpluck[html]
pip install docpluck[all]
```


`extract_pdf()` requires the `pdftotext` binary from Poppler.

- Linux/WSL: `apt-get install poppler-utils`
- macOS: `brew install poppler`
- Windows: install Poppler and add its `bin` folder to `PATH`

## Links

- Full usage and API reference: `docs/README.md`
- Normalization pipeline details: `docs/NORMALIZATION.md`
- Ben

recent_commits.message: feat(tables): region-driven Camelot capture + content-plausibility guards (DP-1/DP-2)
recent_commits.body:
Drive Camelot stream with docpluck's OWN caption-anchored region as
table_areas (per caption) so each extraction is that caption's table by
construction — fixing the stacked-/side-by-side multi-table-per-page class
(efendic Table 4+5, cog_emo) that blind pages="all" auto-detect + token-overlap
pairing could not separate. Region tables are CANDIDATES, not winners:
_pick_better_table keeps the structurally-richer of {region, auto-detect} per
caption (more columns wins + cell-retention guard), so region-driving can only
ADD coverage (0x0 stub -> real table) or replace an auto table it strictly beats
— never collapse a wide table whose region bbox is too narrow (jama_open_1 stays
43x7). A starved caption (the case region-driving exists for) has no auto
candidate, so region wins automatically; no page-multiplicity special-case.

Content-plausibility guards on the region-driven path (keyed on structural
signatures, general to any PDF):
- _cell_is_prose dominance reject: a region that over-captured 2-column body
  text emits a grid whose cells are sentence fragments (cog_emo Table 3 was a
  27x4 of the procedure paragraph). Reject when >=1/3 of rows carry a 6+word
  prose cell -> honest 0x0 stub.
- _CAPTION_LABEL_RE reject: a cell containing "Table N."/"Figure N." proves the
  region absorbed an adjacent caption (cog_emo Table 9 pulled in Table 8's tail +
  its own caption). One occurrence condemns the grid. (Note:/p< NOT rejected —
  legit footnotes, e.g. cog_emo Table 2's matrix.)
Guards are region-only: applying them to the auto-detect path wholesale rejected
legitimate-but-imperfect tables (cog_emo T5/6/7) — a net regression.

Caption page-fix (foundational): advance char_start past leading \n\n/\f
whitespace so captions after a paragraph break / at a page top get a non-empty
line_text (required for _bbox_of_caption_line -> region) and correct page
attribution. Harmless on its own; the old token-overlap pairing it once
perturbed is now superseded by region-driving + _pick_better_table.

Verification (ground truth = AI multimodal PDF/gold reads per project rule, NOT
pdftotext):
- Full 101-PDF structured diff vs v2.4.98 baseline: 27 papers changed, 0 errors.
- AI-gold canary (6 papers): chandrashekar=FIX, ieee_access_4=FIX, efendic page-8
  split=PASS (strict win, values match gold), bmj_open_1=FIX, ieee_access_7=
  NET_BETTER, cog_emo=PASS (both prior regressions fixed, no new ones).
- Full table unit + real-PDF test suite green.

Pre-existing issues surfaced for follow-up (not introduced here): cog_emo Table 8
content-mislabel, efendic Table 2 raw_text-fallback / Table 1 row-into-header,
bmc_med_3 two-fragment pairing, chandrashekar caption cross-column leak, U+2212
(cid)->2 minus artifact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: fix(tables): v2.4.98 — DP-3 η²p-from-structure, DP-5 r-cell + bracket-CI (ESCIcheck handoff; flatten-only, AI-gold-verified)
recent_commits.body:
Addresses ESCIcheckapp/docs/DOCPLUCK_HANDOFF_2026-06-25.md. Three flatten/cell-cleaning
fixes that ship; the DP-1/DP-2 capture fix was prototyped, failed the AI-gold canary
verify (net-harmful corpus-wide), and is reverted + queued. TABLE_EXTRACTION_VERSION
2.4.3 -> 2.4.4. No capture-path change — caption→table pairing byte-identical to v2.4.3.

DP-3 (η²p) — table side FIXED, text side WON'T-FIX. The η²p glyph has no ToUnicode
(pdffonts: NotoSerif uni:no), so pdftotext AND pdfplumber decode it as a space — the
effect-column header is blank and no eta token survives, so flatten fell back to generic
`est`. flatten._infer_anova_eta2_hint now types an unlabeled estimate column as `eta2`
on the structural signature (F-test/ANOVA table: F + BF01/CI, no competing d/r/OR),
range-guarded to [0,1]. collabra.90203 T8/T9 now emit eta2 matching the gold's η²p
column. Body-text η²p is OCR-tier won't-fix (glyph identity absent).

DP-5b — flatten._inline_stat_field types a self-labeled cell (`r = .67`, `d = 0.32`) by
its token even under a generic "Effect size" header; stores the numeric part only so the
sentence renders `r = 0.63` not `r = r = .67`. cog_emo T8 correlations now carry typed r.

DP-5a — cell_cleaning._is_fragment_cell recognizes a bracketed-CI close tail (`0.73]`)
so a CI Camelot split across two rows rejoins (`[0.59, 0.73]`) and the junk fragment row
is dropped (cog_emo T8 14 -> 10 rows).

Pre-existing test-gap fix (leave-nothing-behind): test_table_html_renders_when_structured
and test_table_kinds_are_valid asserted a non-structured table must be kind=="isolated",
but the layout-channel `whitespace` fallback (valid since the v2.4.3 foundation) can
surface — including when Camelot returns "no tables" under cumulative suite load. Tests
now accept `whitespace` (cells + HTML present, no Camelot confidence), so a Camelot
load-flake on jama/ieee/chen fixtures no longer mis-fails.

DEFERRED (surfaced, not dropped) — DP-1/DP-2 capture recovery. The caption page-fix
(find_caption_matches advancing char_start past the leading \f its ^\s* ate) corrects
the off-by-one page attribution and unblocks collabra.77859 Table 4 in isolation, but
the mandatory AI-gold canary verify showed it net-harmful: populating the empty
line_text re-scores same-page caption matching and swaps efendic T4/T5 + cog_emo T8/T9,
half-fixes plos_med. All unit tests stayed green — only AI-verify caught it. Reverted;
needs same-page-caption disambiguation + whitespace region quality gating first. See
docs/TRIAGE_2026-06-25_escicheck_handoff_defects.md + LESSONS L-010.

Verification: full library suite green (pytest -n10: 1818 passed, 0 failed); real-PDF
Camelot tests flake only under serial cumulative load (pass per-file / under xdist).
6-paper AI-gold canary verify confirms DP-3 + DP-5 correct against the reading/stats
golds (and is what drove the DP-1/DP-2 revert).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: feat(tables): RC-T char-level column-detection fallback for tight-kerned tables (v2.4.98, foundation)
recent_commits.body:
The layout-channel foundation for RC-T table-data recovery. On tight-kerned PDFs
pdfplumber's word grouper glues a whole numeric row into ONE token (ip_feldman
Table 10: `.29***−.21***.07`), so whitespace_cells' word-gap column detector finds
no gaps and returns [] -> the table is lost to a caption-only stub. The chars are
still cleanly column-separated by large inter-column gaps; recover from them.

- tables.whitespace.char_whitespace_cells: char-level absolute-x-gap column
  detector, wired as a fallback inside whitespace_cells that fires ONLY when the
  word path finds < 2 columns. Votes on column-START edges (a right-aligned
  numeric table has a variable-width label column -> gap midpoints scatter, but
  the data columns are left-aligned to fixed x), buckets boundaries within 8pt,
  and reinserts intra-cell word spacing from glyph geometry. Recovers all 7 of
  ip_feldman Table 10's regression-coefficient rows matching the AI gold.
- STRICTLY ADDITIVE: the word path (_find_stable_column_boundaries bucket_pt=0)
  is restored VERBATIM to its pre-change behavior, so any table that already
  extracts correctly is byte-unchanged; only a region that yielded NO whitespace
  grid can now gain one, and only above a 60%-row char-column stability bar.
- TABLE_EXTRACTION_VERSION 2.4.2 -> 2.4.3; __version__/pyproject 2.4.97 -> 2.4.98.
  NOT tagged — this is foundation accumulating on main (like v2.4.96/97 did);
  the next release carries it.

NOT yet wired to close the T10 canary: T10's caption is MATCHED by the degenerate
full-page Camelot table, which is used directly, so the whitespace fallback is
never reached for it. Closing it needs an extract_structured.py change (detect a
degenerate matched Camelot table -> discard -> re-extract via whitespace_cells on
the gutter-band-clipped region + a region prose-trim), which touches the core
extraction path for all papers and requires the full ~48-paper guard-diff +
7-canary AI-verify before shipping. Documented in LEARNINGS 2026-06-25 + the RC-T
spec; queued as the next focused cycle (rule 0e-bis — surfaced, not dropped).

Tests: tests/test_whitespace_char_fallback.py (5 cases incl. real-PDF ip_feldman
Table 10 = all 7 gold rows recovered; synthetic tight-kerned recovery; word-space
reinsertion; delegation wiring; single-column-no-fabrication guard). 15/15 pass
on the whitespace/char/caption-table suite (two independent runs); 0 failures
across the broad table/render suite; word path proven byte-identical.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: docs(iterate): 2026-06-25 run handoff + LEARNINGS + project lessons (PARTIAL)
recent_commits.body:
Cycle-1 investigation+verification run. Records:
- Branch reconciliation: main was 4 commits behind its own pushed v2.4.96/97 tags
  (prod ran v2.4.97 while mainline claimed v2.4.95); FF'd to v2.4.97.
- RC-T table-data recovery confirmed == the RC-1 banded layout-channel work
  (architectural, multi-session); 4 canary table/sidebar FAILs remain OPEN.
- RC-1 banded default-flip ATTEMPTED + REJECTED by 8-canary AI-verify (3
  ON_REGRESSION the 26/26 word-preservation scan was blind to).
- 2 project lessons (branch-can-lag-its-own-tags; word-preservation-blind-to-

title block out of order, an affiliation line can land past the H1->first-`##`

corrupting the Abstract's section structure.

paper identity): `_promote_isolated_titlecase_subsection_headings` consults a

("Department/School/Faculty/Division/Institute/Centre/Center/Laboratory/College/
University of <Cap>") or institution-terminated ("X University/College/Institute/
Hospital/Polytechnic") line. The line stays body text (a strict improvement over
a fake heading; affiliation text preserved, never dropped). Guard runs before
the chain-promotion bypass so it holds on every path.

Free, open-source AI academic paper reviewer that outperforms popular paid AI reviewers.

You provide your own API key and pay the LLM provider directly — typically **under $2 per review**.

Don't want to run it locally? Use the [web interface](https://coarse.vercel.app/) instead.

```bash
uvx coarse-ink review paper.pdf --api-key sk-or-v1-YOUR_KEY
```

That's it. The review is written to `paper_review.md` in the current directory.

recent_commits.message: fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244)
recent_commits.body:
Two multilingual fast-follows reported after the v1.8.0 launch:

1. The submit-time "your paper is being reviewed" confirmation email was
   hardcoded English. Localize it to the chosen site_language (the only
   language known at submit time) via web/src/lib/confirmationEmail.ts (12
   locales, English fallback; terminology matches the completion email).
   Placeholders substituted with a function replacer so a filename with
   "$&" can't corrupt output.

2. A review opened from its email link showed English chrome on another
   device (no localStorage). The review page now applies the review's
   stored language once it loads, via a non-persisting applyInitialLocale
   on the site-language hook (an explicit user switch still wins; the
   global preference is never overwritten). New coerceSiteLocale helper.

Web-only — no src/coarse change, no PyPI impact. Coverage in
tests/test_web_confirmation_email.py.

recent_commits.message: fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243)
recent_commits.body:
Supabase Security Advisor flagged public.schema_migrations as exposed to
PostgREST without row-level security. It is internal migration bookkeeping
written only by the service-role path (apply_migrations.sh / SQL editor),
which bypasses RLS, so enabling RLS with no policies locks it from the
anon/authenticated API while migrations keep working — matching the
project's other internal tables. Added at both creation sites (idempotent),
so re-running either secures an existing project.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Local $0 experiment (4 Opus-4.8 reviewers, one per output language;
scored with the production quote verifier + an English-only judge) plus

(Design A) does not degrade review quality and that English-pivot +

write-up, raw data, and a re-runnable quote-fidelity scorer.

First, behavior-neutral step of the multilingual rollout
(docs/MULTILINGUAL_PLAN.md): add the language contract end-to-end
(DB -> web routes -> worker) so later PRs can detect paper language and
localize the review. No behavior change -- every field is nullable/optional
and unset = today's output.

- reviews: + site_language, review_language, paper_language,
  paper_language_source, text_direction (ltr/rtl check) + indexes; idempotent
  deploy/migrate_i18n_contract.sql. Reuses result_json (no review_json);
  taxonomy already existed.
- LanguageContext (src/coarse/types.py) + TS mirror; review_language defaults
  empty = "follow detected paper language" (resolved in the worker later).
- submit + mcp-finalize routes accept/normalize/validate language via a shared
  BCP-47-aware web/src/lib/language.ts (preserves zh-Hant/pt-BR casing); submit
  forwards review_language/site_language to the Modal worker (ReviewRequest).
- review/[id] SELECT unions the new columns and keeps result_json.
- analysis_language intentionally omitted (no consumer under the MVP design).

Tests: tests/test_types.py (LanguageContext), tests/test_web_i18n_contract.py.

_new_commits.message: fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244)
_new_commits.body:
Two multilingual fast-follows reported after the v1.8.0 launch:

1. The submit-time "your paper is being reviewed" confirmation email was
   hardcoded English. Localize it to the chosen site_language (the only
   language known at submit time) via web/src/lib/confirmationEmail.ts (12
   locales, English fallback; terminology matches the completion email).
   Placeholders substituted with a function replacer so a filename with
   "$&" can't corrupt output.

2. A review opened from its email link showed English chrome on another
   device (no localStorage). The review page now applies the review's
   stored language once it loads, via a non-persisting applyInitialLocale
   on the site-language hook (an explicit user switch still wins; the
   global preference is never overwritten). New coerceSiteLocale helper.

Web-only — no src/coarse change, no PyPI impact. Coverage in
tests/test_web_confirmation_email.py.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
_new_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

_new_commits.message: fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243)
_new_commits.body:
Supabase Security Advisor flagged public.schema_migrations as exposed to
PostgREST without row-level security. It is internal migration bookkeeping
written only by the service-role path (apply_migrations.sh / SQL editor),
which bypasses RLS, so enabling RLS with no policies locks it from the
anon/authenticated API while migrations keep working — matching the
project's other internal tables. Added at both creation sites (idempotent),
so re-running either secures an existing project.

A metadata and text extraction and text manipulation tool set for the statistical programming language [R](www.r-project.org). 

**JATSdecoder** facilitates text mining projects on scientific articles by enabling an individual selection of metadata and text parts. 
Its function `JATSdecoder()` extracts metadata, sectioned text and reference list from [NISO-JATS](https://jats.nlm.nih.gov/publishing/tag-library/1.1d2/index.html) coded XML files.

repos.readme_excerpt:
Auto Z-Curve reads a folder of PDF articles, uses Google's Gemini AI to extract focal statistical results from each one, and produces a z-curve analysis report — including a plot, summary statistics, and a full disclosure table.

You interact with it through a simple terminal interface: point it at your PDFs, enter your API key, and click Run.

In the below screenshot, I z-curved 45 articles on cognitive dissonance in under 4 minutes, costing under 2 USD in tokens.

![](assets/example.svg)

**Note:** I have validated statistic extraction as part of a study, which is currently under peer review

repos.readme_excerpt:
OIF (Output It Forward) is a Chrome extension that helps researchers detect and document open data indicators in scholarly articles while they browse. As you read a paper, OIF automatically scans the page for phrases and repository links commonly associated with open data practices, and logs matches to a running CSV file for systematic documentation.
OIF is designed with adaptability in mind: its phrase list and repository domain list are plain, editable text files, so tailoring the extension to your discipline requires no programming expertise, just edit a list.

Follow this link: https://chr

highlights.message: feat(tables): region-driven Camelot capture + content-plausibility guards (DP-1/DP-2)
highlights.body:
Drive Camelot stream with docpluck's OWN caption-anchored region as
table_areas (per caption) so each extraction is that caption's table by
construction — fixing the stacked-/side-by-side multi-table-per-page class
(efendic Table 4+5, cog_emo) that blind pages="all" auto-detect + token-overlap
pairing could not separate. Region tables are CANDIDATES, not winners:
_pick_better_table keeps the structurally-richer of {region, auto-detect} per
caption (more columns wins + cell-retention guard), so region-driving can only
ADD coverage (0x0 stub -> real table) or replace an auto table it strictly beats
— never collapse a wide table whose region bbox is too narrow (jama_open_1 stays
43x7). A starved caption (the case region-driving exists for) has no auto
candidate, so region wins automatically; no page-multiplicity special-case.

Content-plausibility guards on the region-driven path (keyed on structural
signatures, general to any PDF):
- _cell_is_prose dominance reject: a region that over-captured 2-column body
  text emits a grid whose cells are sentence fragments (cog_emo Table 3 was a
  27x4 of the procedure paragraph). Reject when >=1/3 of rows carry a 6+word
  prose cell -> honest 0x0 stub.
- _CAPTION_LABEL_RE reject: a cell containing "Table N."/"Figure N." proves the
  region absorbed an adjacent caption (cog_emo Table 9 pulled in Table 8's tail +
  its own caption). One occurrence condemns the grid. (Note:/p< NOT rejected —
  legit footnotes, e.g. cog_emo Table 2's matrix.)
Guards are region-only: applying them to the auto-detect path wholesale rejected
legitimate-but-imperfect tables (cog_emo T5/6/7) — a net regression.

Caption page-fix (foundational): advance char_start past leading \n\n/\f
whitespace so captions after a paragraph break / at a page top get a non-empty
line_text (required for _bbox_of_caption_line -> region) and correct page
attribution. Harmless on its own; the old token-overlap pairing it once
perturbed is now superseded by region-driving + _pick_better_table.

Verification (ground truth = AI multimodal PDF/gold reads per project rule, NOT
pdftotext):
- Full 101-PDF structured diff vs v2.4.98 baseline: 27 papers changed, 0 errors.
- AI-gold canary (6 papers): chandrashekar=FIX, ieee_access_4=FIX, efendic page-8
  split=PASS (strict win, values match gold), bmj_open_1=FIX, ieee_access_7=
  NET_BETTER, cog_emo=PASS (both prior regressions fixed, no new ones).
- Full table unit + real-PDF test suite green.

Pre-existing issues surfaced for follow-up (not introduced here): cog_emo Table 8
content-mislabel, efendic Table 2 raw_text-fallback / Table 1 row-into-header,
bmc_med_3 two-fragment pairing, chandrashekar caption cross-column leak, U+2212
(cid)->2 minus artifact.

highlights.message: Rewrite OSF prereg extraction to schema-driven dispatch
highlights.body:
Replace the template-name if/else dispatch and per-template parsers
(osf_pr_28/31, oer, prc, prap, osfpre, rrbrandt) with a single
schema-driven extractor:

- dispatch on the stable registration schema_id, with a dedicated
  handler only for the deeply-nested van 't Veer pages schema
- generic osf_pr_schema() reads field labels from the registration's
  schema (blocks display_text or pages question titles) and maps them
  to canonical prereg_schema fields via osf_label_field, with a slug
  fallback so unknown labels are not dropped
- handles both blocks- and pages-format schemas

Add tests for blocks/pages extraction and the van 't Veer handler, with
OSF guid/registration/schema httptest2 fixtures.
highlights.author: Lakens

highlights.message: fix(tables): v2.4.98 — DP-3 η²p-from-structure, DP-5 r-cell + bracket-CI (ESCIcheck handoff; flatten-only, AI-gold-verified)
highlights.body:
Addresses ESCIcheckapp/docs/DOCPLUCK_HANDOFF_2026-06-25.md. Three flatten/cell-cleaning
fixes that ship; the DP-1/DP-2 capture fix was prototyped, failed the AI-gold canary
verify (net-harmful corpus-wide), and is reverted + queued. TABLE_EXTRACTION_VERSION
2.4.3 -> 2.4.4. No capture-path change — caption→table pairing byte-identical to v2.4.3.

DP-3 (η²p) — table side FIXED, text side WON'T-FIX. The η²p glyph has no ToUnicode
(pdffonts: NotoSerif uni:no), so pdftotext AND pdfplumber decode it as a space — the
effect-column header is blank and no eta token survives, so flatten fell back to generic
`est`. flatten._infer_anova_eta2_hint now types an unlabeled estimate column as `eta2`
on the structural signature (F-test/ANOVA table: F + BF01/CI, no competing d/r/OR),
range-guarded to [0,1]. collabra.90203 T8/T9 now emit eta2 matching the gold's η²p
column. Body-text η²p is OCR-tier won't-fix (glyph identity absent).

DP-5b — flatten._inline_stat_field types a self-labeled cell (`r = .67`, `d = 0.32`) by
its token even under a generic "Effect size" header; stores the numeric part only so the
sentence renders `r = 0.63` not `r = r = .67`. cog_emo T8 correlations now carry typed r.

DP-5a — cell_cleaning._is_fragment_cell recognizes a bracketed-CI close tail (`0.73]`)
so a CI Camelot split across two rows rejoins (`[0.59, 0.73]`) and the junk fragment row
is dropped (cog_emo T8 14 -> 10 rows).

Pre-existing test-gap fix (leave-nothing-behind): test_table_html_renders_when_structured
and test_table_kinds_are_valid asserted a non-structured table must be kind=="isolated",
but the layout-channel `whitespace` fallback (valid since the v2.4.3 foundation) can
surface — including when Camelot returns "no tables" under cumulative suite load. Tests
now accept `whitespace` (cells + HTML present, no Camelot confidence), so a Camelot
load-flake on jama/ieee/chen fixtures no longer mis-fails.

DEFERRED (surfaced, not dropped) — DP-1/DP-2 capture recovery. The caption page-fix
(find_caption_matches advancing char_start past the leading \f its ^\s* ate) corrects
the off-by-one page attribution and unblocks collabra.77859 Table 4 in isolation, but
the mandatory AI-gold canary verify showed it net-harmful: populating the empty
line_text re-scores same-page caption matching and swaps efendic T4/T5 + cog_emo T8/T9,
half-fixes plos_med. All unit tests stayed green — only AI-verify caught it. Reverted;
needs same-page-caption disambiguation + whitespace region quality gating first. See
docs/TRIAGE_2026-06-25_escicheck_handoff_defects.md + LESSONS L-010.

Verification: full library suite green (pytest -n10: 1818 passed, 0 failed); real-PDF
Camelot tests flake only under serial cumulative load (pass per-file / under xdist).
6-paper AI-gold canary verify confirms DP-3 + DP-5 correct against the reading/stats
golds (and is what drove the DP-1/DP-2 revert).

highlights.message: feat(tables): RC-T char-level column-detection fallback for tight-kerned tables (v2.4.98, foundation)
highlights.body:
The layout-channel foundation for RC-T table-data recovery. On tight-kerned PDFs
pdfplumber's word grouper glues a whole numeric row into ONE token (ip_feldman
Table 10: `.29***−.21***.07`), so whitespace_cells' word-gap column detector finds
no gaps and returns [] -> the table is lost to a caption-only stub. The chars are
still cleanly column-separated by large inter-column gaps; recover from them.

- tables.whitespace.char_whitespace_cells: char-level absolute-x-gap column
  detector, wired as a fallback inside whitespace_cells that fires ONLY when the
  word path finds < 2 columns. Votes on column-START edges (a right-aligned
  numeric table has a variable-width label column -> gap midpoints scatter, but
  the data columns are left-aligned to fixed x), buckets boundaries within 8pt,
  and reinserts intra-cell word spacing from glyph geometry. Recovers all 7 of
  ip_feldman Table 10's regression-coefficient rows matching the AI gold.
- STRICTLY ADDITIVE: the word path (_find_stable_column_boundaries bucket_pt=0)
  is restored VERBATIM to its pre-change behavior, so any table that already
  extracts correctly is byte-unchanged; only a region that yielded NO whitespace
  grid can now gain one, and only above a 60%-row char-column stability bar.
- TABLE_EXTRACTION_VERSION 2.4.2 -> 2.4.3; __version__/pyproject 2.4.97 -> 2.4.98.
  NOT tagged — this is foundation accumulating on main (like v2.4.96/97 did);
  the next release carries it.

NOT yet wired to close the T10 canary: T10's caption is MATCHED by the degenerate
full-page Camelot table, which is used directly, so the whitespace fallback is
never reached for it. Closing it needs an extract_structured.py change (detect a
degenerate matched Camelot table -> discard -> re-extract via whitespace_cells on
the gutter-band-clipped region + a region prose-trim), which touches the core
extraction path for all papers and requires the full ~48-paper guard-diff +
7-canary AI-verify before shipping. Documented in LEARNINGS 2026-06-25 + the RC-T
spec; queued as the next focused cycle (rule 0e-bis — surfaced, not dropped).

Tests: tests/test_whitespace_char_fallback.py (5 cases incl. real-PDF ip_feldman
Table 10 = all 7 gold rows recovered; synthetic tight-kerned recovery; word-space
reinsertion; delegation wiring; single-column-no-fabrication guard). 15/15 pass
on the whitespace/char/caption-table suite (two independent runs); 0 failures
across the broad table/render suite; word path proven byte-identical.

highlights.message: fix(tooling): direction-aware check_app_pin_sync note + RC-T/RC-1 spec findings
highlights.body:
scripts/check_app_pin_sync.py::compare() printed "working-tree X is ahead of
latest tag Y -- UNRELEASED. Tag + push X" for ANY version != tag, so a checkout
BEHIND its own latest released tag (a stale 2.4.95 working copy while v2.4.97 is
the live release) was wrongly told it was "ahead" and advised to tag an OLDER
version. That advice is actively harmful and was hit live during this session's
branch reconciliation (main was 4 commits behind its own pushed v2.4.96/97 tags).
Made the note direction-aware via an ordered _vtuple() comparison: ahead ->
UNRELEASED-tag hint; behind -> loud stale-checkout WARNING; equal -> no note.
New tests/test_check_app_pin_sync.py (10 cases) regression-guards the behind-case
(fails-before / passes-after) + ahead/equal/mismatch/malformed.

Docs (investigation findings for the next session, no library code change):
- RC-T spec: Layer-1 table-data recovery confirmed == the RC-1 banded layout-
  channel work (probed 4 angles; ip_feldman T10's 7 rows lost vs gold; x-gutter
  evidence shows the table IS x-separable but tight-kerned glue + interleave-in-
  region defeat both Camelot and whitespace_cells). Orphan ### Table N heading is
  the DELIBERATE v2.4.55 clean-fail, not the fix.
- RC-1 spec: banded default-flip ATTEMPTED and REJECTED by 8-canary AI-verify
  (3 ON_REGRESSION: ar_apa furniture-injection + section-inversion, chan_feldman
  cross-section bleed, maier single-column false-positive). The 26/26 word-
  preservation scan MISSED all 3 (blind to reordering) -> validates AI-verify-
  mandatory. Flag stays default-OFF. Precise gap-5/6/7 blockers + a stricter
  zero-regression re-verify gate recorded for the next session.

No docpluck/ library code changed -> no version bump (tooling + docs + test).

highlights.body:
rtransparent_coi() used the magrittr pipe %>%, which is only available
when dplyr is attached, not when it is merely loaded as module_run() does.
Running coi_check as the first dplyr-importing module in a fresh session
therefore errored. Replace the two %>% with the base pipe |>.

Also simplify the table pipeline in both modules from a
nest_by/rowwise/mutate(data$text) dance to summarise(.by = paper_id),
removing the fragile rowwise data pronoun.

digest.narrative: Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. In the `scienceverse/metacheck` project, the `ref_update` branch has been the focus of attention, with updates to `ref_accuracy`, `reg_check`, and `ref_consistency`, as well as improvements to error logging. Meanwhile, `Davidvandijcke/coarse` has made strides in localization and deployment fixes, while `JamieCummins/regcheck` has removed the sign-up feature from its `staging` branch. Notably, `giladfeldman/escicheck` has synchronized changes from the `escicheckapp` repository, bringing in updates from `effectcheck` version `0.6.11`. These connections between projects demonstrate the collaborative spirit of our community, and we celebrate the progress being made towards our shared goals. As our projects continue to evolve, we look forward to seeing the impact of these updates and the new developments on the horizon.
